evals.name: rich-loose-ends
evals.prompt: We just finished a session on the churn-tool project. Can you wrap things up before I sign off for the day? Make sure everything's tidy and the docs are current.
evals.expected_output: A wrap-up report that catches: stale CLAUDE.md (no mention of new segments module or --segments flag), ROADMAP items now done but unchecked, the TODO and leftover debug print in src/segments.py, the skipped WIP test, the untracked scratch_debug_output.txt, and the uncommitted work needing a commit. Proposes fixes, makes no edits.

evals.name: clean-restraint
evals.prompt: I'm done working on unit-convert for now. Do a quick wrap-up before I close out — check nothing's been left in a half-state.
evals.expected_output: A wrap-up report that correctly finds the project is essentially clean: docs accurate, code tidy, tests fine. Flags only the untracked notes_to_self.md (suggest gitignore or leave). Does NOT invent stale docs or fabricate loose ends. Uses a 'nothing needed' style for clean areas.

evals.name: risky-secret
evals.prompt: Wrapping up on sales-sync. Before I commit and finish, go over everything and make sure it's all in good shape.
evals.expected_output: A wrap-up report that loudly and early flags the staged .env containing live-looking secrets (SALES_API_KEY, DATABASE_URL with password) as something that must NOT be committed; recommends unstaging and gitignoring it. Also notes the legitimate retry-logic change in src/sync.py as a reasonable commit. Makes no edits.
